rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT WITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a fourth report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet, as ordered on 1/11/23. No Corrected I&A has been filed yet. The purported "Final" I&A filed on 2/1/19 claimed that there was $70,000 in cash but that was described as "income from estate property earned during probate of estate," which is clearly not cash on hand as of the date of death. Executor has reportedly changed legal counsel again but is currently still self-represented. The Court might consider removing Executor and appointing the Public Administrator, or, if the Public Administrator declines in favor of a creditor, the creditor could be appointed as administrator with will annexed in order to complete estate administration. [Probate Code Sections 12204 and 8461.] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE PATRICK WILLIAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: 1. Petition needs to be amended to allege a specific period for which administration need to be continued. PC sec.12201(a). LRSF 14.35.J.(2). 2. Amended Petition to specify "Third" report in its caption. CRC 7.102. 3. Even though petition includes some accounting information, this petition is treated as report of status of administration. 4. Need Schedule B of Receipt. 5. Need Schedule C of disbursements. 6. Proposed order must be submitted before hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD COLE WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 9/25/23 is for just the amendment that was filed on 9/1/23, not also for the petition that it amended. No notice of the "First Two-Year Account and Petition for Approval" has been served yet. (2) The ONLY I&A filed in this matter (on 5/9/22) showed the estate appraised at $1,174,257.89, not $1,103,178.07, as alleged in paragraph 10 of the petition and still in the amendment filed on 9/1/23. No Corrected I&A has been filed yet. (3) The account attached to the amendment is virtually the same as the one attached to the original petition. There is still no schedule showing assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period, as required by Probate Code Section 1061(a)(1). (4) Why doesn't Schedule D (Property on Hand) show the total FMV? (5) All accounts at the beginning of an account in a decedent's estate should be in the decedent's name, but financial institution statements verifying the balances on hand at the END of the accounting should show accounts held in an ESTATE account. (6) The brokerage account statements still do not show that assets have been transferred to an ESTATE account. (7) The disability check is still on hand more than two years after Letters were issued to the executor Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORRIS ROHDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Net Proceeds From Sales Of Real Property To Sister State Personal Representative
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues need to be cleared: (1) Petition must be verified by Administrator who is the court appointed fiduciary with the duty to make the report, not by the fiduciary's attorney, whether the fiduciary is absent in this County. PC 1021(b)(2) and PC 1021(b)(2) and CRC 7.103.(c).Petitioner's attorney continues to verify documents; however, a petition is not properly before the court until the fiduciary's verification is filed unless petition presents an authority under which the court should allow the fiduciary's attorney's verification. PC 1023. (1) A certified copy of Letters from the Washington County, Texas Court. It needs to be filed conventionally pursuant to LRSF 14.59.C. (2) Notice of Hearing to all persons who are entitled and the notice to include Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. PC 11000 and LRSF 14.1.D.6. Notice of hearing on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORRIS ROHDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues need to be cleared: (1) Petition must be verified by Administrator who is the court appointed fiduciary with the duty to make the report, not by the fiduciary's attorney, whether the fiduciary is absent in this County. Petitioner's attorney filed a separate verification on 10/2/23; however, it does not clear the issue. PC 1021(b)(2) and CRC 7.103.(c). (2) A certified copy of Letters from the Washington County, Texas Court. It needs to be filed conventionally pursuant to LRSF 14.59.C. (3) Notice of Hearing to all persons who are entitled and the notice to include Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled to. PC 11000 and LRSF 14.1.D.6. Notice of hearing filed 8/7/2023 shows the notices were sent to the heirs' counsels' emails; however, no allegation if PC 1220(c) has been met (i.e., consents for e-service not on file). (4) Petition Schedule G shows the total amount; however, it does not provide where the funds are being held and if the funds are fully insured. (5) Financial statement showing the balance. PC 10900. (6) A separate declaration for extraordinary compensation pursuant to CRC 7.702. Petition seeks the compensation $14,095; however, no relevant facts have been provided. (7) Description of the cost advanced by the counsel in the total amount of $3,456.99. Petition seeks the reimbursement; however, no relevant information provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/29/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) As with the first accounting, which was AMENDED by a "supplement declaration" filed on 3/14/22, before a proper amendment was filed on 5/5/22, this "Declaration" filed on 9/27/23 AMENDS the current petition without properly being captioned as the AMENDMENT that it is. (2) An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. (3) The tangible personal property (the specifically devised Asian ceramics and painting and artwork) were shown on Schedule D (Losses) of the "Supplement to Account" attached to the amendment filed on 5/5/22. Neither Schedule E nor Schedule F of that amended first account included these assets on hand at the end of that accounting period. (That first accounting was not approved; it was merely acknowledged as having been filed.) (4) Notwithstanding Executor's explanation about how he has handled the storage of the tangible personal property appraised at a total of $4,000, he could have filed a Notice of Proposed Action to distribute them to the devisees so that the estate (or, ultimately, James Ray) does not have to bear the cost - which was $9,085.45 during this accounting period alone. [Probate Code Section 10520.] (5) Alternatively, this second accounting petition could have requested preliminary distribution. (6) It is unclear how the bank statements attached as Exhibit: Note "6" explain the difference between the $24,000 the executor reportedly advanced to the estate and the $25,749.00 in repayments he disbursed to himself. There appears to be a deposit of $5,000. What does this represent? (7) Please file invoices or bills substantiating the $3,925 that the executor advanced for property maintenance and utility expenses. (8) If Claiborne Iavoie Associates was paid $1,050.00 on 3/3/21 for preparation of estate income tax returns, for what tax preparation was Karen Johnson paid $1,146.00 on 6/14/21? (9) Attorney costs paid during this accounting period totaled $2,200.48. Note "10" attached to the "Declaration" shows costs totaling $2,143.84. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The attorneys represented the beneficiary's mother as his guardian ad litem and now represent the trustee of this PSNT. How is this not a conflict? (2) The instructions regarding remote appearance procedures was not mailed until 9/19/23, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. (In future, please just attach the instructions to the Notice of Hearing.) (3) The trustee (or the beneficiary's mother?) believes luxury men's suits and shoes from Bloomingdale's, among many other purchases totaling nearly 20% of the initial funding of the trust, are "reasonable"? (4) Photocopies of photos of receipts on a countertop or desk are illegible. (Why were they not scanned?) (5) Paragraph 10 of the trust officer's declaration filed on 9/27/23 does not explain the market value changes. (6) Does Gabriele Keezer qualify to bill as a paralegal? [Bus. And Prof. Code Section 6450.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct information. [CRC 7.3.] Issues posted in the tentative ruling should not be "responded" to. Each filed paper should stand on its own and simply supplement or amend the petition.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANAE ANNE MANUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The decedent was alleged in the initial Petition for Letters of Administration to have died on 8/18/22, not 8/8/22. The administrator is reportedly the sister, so references to "him" or "his" will be disregarded, as will the two contradictory paragraphs 17 of the petition, the allegation that all personal property taxes have been paid, and the allegation that all duties have been performed by the executor of Decedent's will. Ordinarily, when the net cash is less than the net sale proceeds + cash on hand as of the date of death, some explanation should be given in a petition for final distribution. The administrator is Decedent's sole heir and only beneficiary of this estate; there was no need for her to waive her own accounting. NB: NEW instructions for how to appear remotely are available on the court's website. Counsel should update the instructions attached to notices going forward.

If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 10/22/24, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARIO DELUCCHI AND KATHLEEN DELUCCHI FAMILY TRUSTUTD APRIL 14, 1987, AS AMENDED AND ESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition: 1. Recovery Of Trust Property; For Double Damages; 2. For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of The Independednt Successor Trustee 3. To Compel Trustee To Account And Report 4. Permanent Injuction 5. Partition By Division Or Slae Of Real Property 6. For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/23 for processing of documents in e-filing queue. In addition: 1) The notice filed on 9/25/23 does not provide 30 day notice. Also, c/o notice is insufficient; direct notice must be provided unless a copy of the power of attorney is filed showing that the agent is authorized to receive service. 2) Petitioner alleges that various beneficiaries of the trust were not listed and given notice because they could not be located. Petitioner further alleges they do not need to be given notice as they are not affected by the petition; however, it is unclear whether they would be affected, and no authority has been cited for the proposition that beneficiaries who are not affected by a petition do not need to be noticed. 3) The requests made in the "Supplemental Petition" filed on 9/25/23 are different than those made in the petition. As new requests are being made, an "Amendment to the" petition must be filed and 30 day Notice of Hearing given on its filing. 4) Proposed order has not been submitted, which should be e-lodged. All documents to be filed and viewable on the register of actions by 10/30/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BYRON R. MEYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The names and dates of death of the decedent's parents must be provided. 2) Item 8 of the petition must list the petitioner. 3) All beneficiaries of the devisee trust entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 4) The date of death of Sandra Kass must be provided. If she predeceased the decedent, her issue must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 5) Chester Meyer's issue must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 6) Does the petitioner, as interim trustee of the devisee trust, have authority to nominate himself or other person as administrator with the will annexed under section 8441? 7) Sufficient facts in support of section 8402(a)(3) must be alleged as to the competing petitioner and executor. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BYRON R. MEYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) The petitioner's name stated at items 2 and 3 of the DE-121 form must exactly match the name under which this petition was filed (no middle initial). 6) The petition caption: The first box must be checked, as the petitioner is named as executor in the will. 7) Item 2b(1) must be checked. 8) All beneficiaries of the devisee trust entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 9) The date of death of Sandra Kass must be provided. If she predeceased the decedent, her issue must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 10) Chester Meyer's issue must be clearly identified as such and listed at item 8. 11) The proof of service attached to the DE-121 form does not show direct notice given to Barbara Meyer. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). No waiver of direct notice signed by her or her attorney is on file. See section 1204. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES G. P. COLLINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, all trust beneficiaries must be listed in item 8 of the petition and must be clearly identified as such. (Item 3 of Attachment 2 to the petition is unclear; the sole beneficiary of this estate is the trustee of the James G.P. Collins Survivor's Trust. Michael Collins is presumably the successor trustee of that devisee trust. He, individually, is not the sole beneficiary of this estate.) (2) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (3) The complete Notice with the language regarding remote appearance procedures was not mailed until 9/27/23, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
